wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the blog management system overview. Explain its purpose as a centralized multi-channel news management platform with role-based access control, architecture overview, and relationship to Supabase backend services. Document the implementation approach using Next.js App Router, TypeScript, and React components. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners explaining multi-channel publishing and role-based permissions, and technical details for experienced developers covering the Supabase integration. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including channels, editors, and news management workflows. Provide practical examples demonstrating the dashboard interface and component integration patterns. Document the technology stack including Next.js 16.2.1, Supabase, React 19.2.4, and Tailwind CSS. Include system architecture diagrams showing the relationship between frontend components, API routes, and Supabase backend services.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the blog management system database schema. Detail the entity relationship diagram showing channels, user_profiles, news, channel_editors, and news_channels tables. Document primary keys (UUID), foreign key relationships, unique constraints, and indexes. Explain the multi-channel publishing architecture with junction table news_channels for many-to-many relationships. Document field definitions including channel properties (name, slug, url, logo_url), user profiles with role-based permissions, news content with status tracking, and channel-editor permissions mapping. Include database schema diagrams with referential integrity constraints. Document data validation rules, business constraints like unique slugs, and cascading behaviors. Specify data lifecycle management, soft deletion patterns, and audit trail fields (created_at, updated_at). Address database performance considerations including indexing strategies for frequently queried columns like slug, status, and timestamps.

wiki_catalogs.name: News Creation and Editing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the news creation and editing functionality. Explain the complete workflow for creating new news articles including form validation, content formatting, and metadata management. Document the createNews function implementation with all required parameters (title, content, excerpt, image_url, channel_id, author_id, slug). Detail the draft creation process and how news items are initially stored with status 'draft'. Explain the updateNews functionality for modifying existing content including partial updates and content revision tracking. Include practical examples of news creation forms, content validation rules, and error handling. Document the relationship between news items and authors through the author_id foreign key. Address content formatting options, image upload handling, and slug generation. Provide guidance on content organization, preview functionality, and collaborative editing workflows. Include troubleshooting tips for common creation/editing issues and best practices for content quality.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the system architecture section focused on the blog management system's technical architecture. Explain the high-level design using Next.js App Router with server-side rendering, Supabase backend services, and React component architecture. Document the layered architecture pattern with presentation layer (Next.js pages/components), business logic layer (lib/data.ts), and data access layer (Supabase client). Include detailed component interaction diagrams showing how frontend components communicate with API routes and Supabase backend services. Explain the cloud-native deployment architecture using Vercel hosting with Supabase managed services. Document the real-time capabilities through Supabase realtime features and how they integrate with the React component lifecycle. Address the separation of concerns between public API endpoints and private admin interfaces. Include technical decision rationale for choosing Next.js App Router over Pages Router, TypeScript for type safety, and Supabase for backend-as-a-service approach.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on quick setup and initial configuration of the blog management system. Explain prerequisites including Node.js 18+, Supabase account requirements, and npm/yarn package manager. Document step-by-step installation process including repository cloning, dependency installation, and Supabase project setup. Detail the database schema execution using supabase-schema.sql, authentication provider configuration, and first super_admin user creation. Explain environment variable configuration with detailed examples for NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, and NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL. Include local development setup with npm run dev and initial verification steps. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues and environment configuration problems. Make content accessible to beginners while ensuring all technical requirements are clearly specified.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the React component architecture and reusable UI components. Document the NewsFeed component with comprehensive props including baseUrl, channelSlug, limit, showImages, and styling options. Explain the NewsWidget component for compact sidebar integration with size variants and customization parameters. Detail component composition patterns, prop interfaces from lib/types.ts, and TypeScript type safety throughout. Document component lifecycle, rendering optimization strategies, and client-server data fetching patterns. Include usage examples for different integration scenarios: Next.js pages, external websites, and third-party frameworks. Explain component state management, error handling, loading states, and fallback mechanisms. Document styling approaches using Tailwind CSS classes, responsive design patterns, and accessibility compliance. Address component reusability, configuration flexibility, and integration with the broader application architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Publication Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the news publication workflow and multi-channel publishing system. Explain the complete publication process including the publishNews function implementation, status transitions from 'draft' to 'published', and timestamp management. Document the multi-channel publishing mechanism through the news_channels junction table and how news items can be distributed across multiple channels simultaneously. Detail the publishNews function parameters including newsId and channelIds array, and explain the upsert operation for news_channels records. Include practical examples of publication workflows, channel assignment processes, and publication scheduling. Explain the relationship between news items and channels through the many-to-many relationship. Address publication approval processes, content moderation workflows, and collaborative publishing scenarios. Document the published_at timestamp management and how it affects content ordering. Provide guidance on publication troubleshooting, channel-specific content variations, and bulk publishing operations. Include best practices for publication timing and content distribution strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the technology stack section focused on the complete tech stack used in the blog management system. Document Next.js 16.2.1 as the primary React framework with App Router for file-based routing and server-side rendering capabilities. Explain TypeScript 5.9.3 integration for type safety across the entire application including interfaces, generics, and compile-time error checking. Detail React 19.2.4 usage with modern hooks, component composition patterns, and concurrent features. Document Tailwind CSS 4.2.2 utility-first styling approach with custom configurations for responsive design and dark theme support. Include Supabase integration covering PostgreSQL database, authentication services, and realtime features. Explain Vercel deployment platform benefits and configuration. Document development tools including ESLint, Prettier, and build optimization. Address version compatibility requirements and upgrade paths. Include performance considerations for each technology choice and how they work together to create a cohesive development experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the blog management system. Describe the high-level design using Next.js App Router with server-side rendering and client-side interactivity. Document component interactions between React components, API routes, data layer, and Supabase backend services. Explain technical decisions including the use of Supabase for authentication, database, and real-time features. Include infrastructure requirements with Vercel deployment integration and cloud-native architecture. Provide system context diagrams showing data flow from user requests through API routes to database operations. Document the layered architecture pattern with presentation layer (React components), business logic layer (data.ts), and data access layer (Supabase client). Address cross-cutting concerns like security through Row Level Security policies, session management, and JWT token handling. Document technology stack integration points and third-party dependencies. Explain scalability considerations and deployment topology for production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the data flow patterns and API layer design. Document the layered architecture with presentation layer (React components), business logic layer (data.ts), and data access layer (Supabase client). Explain the three-tier data flow: user interactions through React components → API routes → database operations. Detail the CRUD operations for news management including create, read, update, and delete workflows. Document the multi-channel publishing pipeline from draft creation to public distribution. Explain the public API data flow from external website requests through API routes to database queries and response formatting. Include request-response patterns, error propagation, and data transformation layers. Document caching strategies, data invalidation patterns, and real-time update mechanisms. Address data validation, sanitization, and security measures at each layer. Explain the separation between authenticated private APIs for administrative functions and public APIs for external integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Status Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the content status management system and workflow states. Explain the complete lifecycle of news items through different status states: draft, published, hidden, and archived. Document how status changes are managed through the updateNews function and the impact of each status on content visibility and distribution. Detail the getPublishedNews function implementation and how it filters content by status 'published' for public consumption. Explain the relationship between status management and user roles, showing how different roles can view and modify content at various stages. Include practical examples of status transitions, visibility controls, and content lifecycle management. Document the status filtering mechanisms used in both private dashboard views and public API responses. Address content approval workflows, editorial review processes, and status-based access controls. Provide guidance on status management best practices, content archiving strategies, and status-based reporting. Include troubleshooting tips for status-related issues and performance considerations for status-heavy content management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Key Features and Capabilities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the key features section focused on the blog management system's core capabilities and functionality. Document the multi-channel news management system that enables organizations to operate multiple independent websites with separate content management. Explain the role-based access control system with super_admin, admin, and editor roles and their specific permissions. Detail the real-time content distribution capabilities through both React components and REST API endpoints. Document the flexible component integration options including NewsFeed and NewsWidget components with customizable display properties. Explain the dual integration approach supporting React/Next.js applications, REST API consumption, and direct browser fetch requests. Include the adaptive design with dark theme support and responsive layouts. Document the collaborative editing workflow with draft saving, content approval processes, and multi-channel publishing capabilities. Address the public API endpoints for external website integration and the component-based integration for seamless third-party website embedding.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Roles and Permissions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the role-based access control system. Explain the three-tier role hierarchy: super_admin with full system administration, admin with channel-specific management, and editor with content creation and editing permissions. Document permission matrix detailing what each role can access and modify across channels, news content, and user management. Detail the implementation of role-based access control through Supabase Row Level Security policies and custom authorization functions. Include practical examples of role assignment, permission checking, and access control enforcement in API routes and components. Explain the user profile management system and how roles are stored and validated. Document the channel_editor relationship table and granular permission settings for individual channels. Provide troubleshooting guidance for permission-related issues and role assignment problems. Address security considerations and best practices for managing user roles in multi-channel environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security architecture documentation covering the multi-layered security approach. Document the four-layer security model: authentication (Supabase Auth with JWT tokens), authorization (role-based access control with RBAC), row-level security (RLS policies), and API security (CORS, rate limiting, input validation). Explain the implementation of Supabase authentication including JWT token management, session handling, and secure cookie configuration. Detail the role-based access control system with super_admin, admin, and editor roles and their specific permissions. Document Row Level Security policies applied to database queries for channel-specific visibility and content access control. Explain granular permissions through channel_editors table with create, edit, delete, and publish capabilities. Include API security measures such as CORS configuration, input validation, SQL injection prevention, and rate limiting strategies. Document session management, token refresh mechanisms, and logout procedures. Address security best practices, threat mitigation strategies, and compliance considerations for the cloud-native deployment architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Multi-Channel Publishing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the multi-channel publishing system and channel management functionality. Explain the many-to-many relationship between news items and channels through the news_channels junction table. Document the publishNews function's handling of multiple channel assignments and the upsert operation for news_channels records. Detail the channel selection process in the dashboard interface and how editors can assign news to multiple channels simultaneously. Include practical examples of channel assignment workflows, channel-specific content variations, and bulk publishing operations. Explain the relationship between channels and their properties (name, slug, url, description) and how they affect content distribution. Document the channel visibility controls and how inactive channels are handled in the publishing process. Address channel-specific content management, custom channel configurations, and channel-based content filtering. Provide guidance on channel assignment best practices, content duplication strategies, and channel-specific metadata management. Include troubleshooting tips for multi-channel publishing issues and performance optimization for large-scale content distribution.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Roles and Permissions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the user roles and permissions section focused on the hierarchical access control system. Document the three-tier role structure: super_admin with full system access including channel management and user administration, admin with channel-specific management capabilities for content creation and publication, and editor with content creation and editing permissions limited to assigned channels. Explain the permission matrix detailing what each role can access and modify, including content creation, editing, deletion, and publishing rights. Document the channel editor assignment system and how permissions are inherited through the channel-editor relationship. Detail the Row Level Security (RLS) policies implemented in Supabase that enforce access control at the database level. Explain the JWT token-based authentication flow and session management. Include practical examples of role assignment scenarios and permission inheritance patterns. Document the security implications of each role and best practices for role management. Address the audit trail capabilities and activity tracking for administrative actions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Content Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on the news content management functionality. Explain the complete workflow for creating, editing, publishing, and managing news articles across multiple channels. Document the news CRUD operations including draft creation, content editing, status management (draft, published, hidden, archived), and multi-channel publishing capabilities. Detail the implementation of the content management system through API routes and data layer functions. Include practical examples of news creation forms, content validation, image handling, and publication workflows. Explain the relationship between news items and channels through the news_channels junction table. Document the content status management system and how different roles can interact with content at various stages. Provide guidance on content moderation, approval workflows, and collaborative editing processes. Address content organization, search functionality, and content lifecycle management. Include troubleshooting guidance for common content management issues and performance optimization tips for large content volumes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Channel Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on multi-channel management functionality. Explain the channel creation process including channel properties (name, slug, URL, description, logo), channel configuration options, and activation/deactivation controls. Document the implementation of channel management through API endpoints and data layer functions. Detail the channel visibility settings, public/private channel configurations, and access control mechanisms. Include practical examples of channel setup, configuration changes, and channel removal procedures. Explain the relationship between channels and news content, including how news items are associated with specific channels. Document the channel editor assignment system and how administrators can grant editing permissions to users. Provide guidance on channel branding, customization options, and channel-specific content organization. Address channel performance considerations, SEO optimization settings, and channel analytics tracking. Include troubleshooting guidance for channel configuration issues and permission problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the React component integration system. Describe the NewsFeed component's visual appearance, behavior, and user interaction patterns for displaying comprehensive news feeds. Document the NewsWidget component's compact design, grid layouts, and sidebar integration capabilities. Detail component props including baseUrl for API communication, channelSlug for filtering, limit for controlling display quantity, and styling options like showImages and showExcerpt. Include comprehensive usage examples with code snippets showing NewsFeed integration in Next.js applications, NewsWidget implementation for sidebars, and custom styling approaches. Provide guidelines for responsive design adaptation, accessibility compliance, and cross-browser compatibility. Document component states, loading indicators, error handling, and empty state management. Include style customization options, theme integration, and Tailwind CSS class overrides. Address component composition patterns, integration with existing UI frameworks, and performance optimization techniques. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common integration issues and component configuration problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the RESTful API endpoints. For the public API, document GET /api/public/news with query parameters channel and limit, and GET /api/public/news/:id for individual news retrieval. For private API endpoints, document POST /api/news for creating news, PUT /api/news/:id for updates, DELETE /api/news/:id for removal, GET /api/channels for channel listing, and POST /api/channels for channel creation restricted to super_admin. Detail request/response schemas including JSON structures for news items, channel objects, and error responses. Document authentication requirements using Supabase JWT tokens and session-based authorization. Include comprehensive examples of API requests and responses with curl commands and JavaScript fetch implementations. Explain rate limiting considerations, error handling strategies, and API versioning approaches. Document common use cases including news feed integration, content management workflows, and third-party website integration. Address API security considerations, CORS configuration, and performance optimization techniques. Provide debugging tools, monitoring approaches, and troubleshooting guidance for API integration issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for this section focused on deployment strategies and production configuration. Explain the recommended Vercel deployment process including GitHub integration, environment variable configuration, and automatic deployment setup. Document Docker deployment options with build commands, container configuration, and environment variable management. Detail traditional hosting deployment using npm run build and npm start commands. Include environment variable configuration for production including Supabase credentials, application URLs, and security settings. Explain domain configuration, SSL certificate setup, and CDN optimization. Document performance optimization techniques including image optimization settings, static generation, and caching strategies. Address monitoring and logging setup, error tracking, and performance monitoring. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common deployment issues, environment configuration problems, and production performance optimization. Include security best practices for production environments, firewall configuration, and access control setup.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting content addressing common issues and frequently asked questions. Document typical setup problems including environment variable misconfiguration, database connection issues, and authentication failures. Provide step-by-step solutions for common runtime errors including permission denied messages, API endpoint failures, and component integration problems. Include debugging strategies for database queries, API request/response issues, and frontend component rendering problems. Document performance troubleshooting including slow API responses, database query optimization, and frontend performance issues. Address security-related troubleshooting including authentication problems, authorization failures, and Row Level Security policy conflicts. Provide guidance for error message interpretation, log analysis, and diagnostic procedures. Include community support resources, issue reporting procedures, and escalation paths. Document known limitations, workarounds for common scenarios, and migration guidance for system upgrades. Address backup and recovery procedures, data integrity verification, and disaster recovery planning.

wiki_overview.content:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the project. Let me generate the detailed analysis.

<blog>

# Blog Management System - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
The Blog Management System is a multi-channel news management platform built with Next.js and Supabase. It enables organizations to manage multiple news channels, assign editors with granular permissions, and publish content across various websites through a unified API.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Multi-channel Content Management**: Support multiple news sources with independent branding and content
- **Role-based Access Control**: Implement hierarchical permissions (super_admin, admin, editor) with channel-specific access
- **Real-time Content Distribution**: Provide public APIs for external websites to display news feeds
- **Scalable Architecture**: Built on modern web technologies with cloud-native deployment capabilities

### Target Audience
- News organizations managing multiple websites
- Content management teams requiring collaborative editing workflows
- Technical teams seeking a scalable, serverless content management solution

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Applications] --> B[Next.js Frontend]
    B --> C[API Routes]
    C --> D[Supabase Client]
    D --> E[(PostgreSQL Database)]
    
    F[External Websites] --> G[Public API]
    G --> D
    
    H[Admin Dashboard] --> B
    I[Content Components] --> B
```

### Design Patterns
- **Repository Pattern**: Data access abstraction through dedicated functions
- **API Route Pattern**: Server-side routes for data operations
- **Component Composition**: Reusable UI components with flexible props
- **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)**: Hierarchical permission system

### System Relationships

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Authentication] --> B[User Profiles]
    B --> C[Channel Editors]
    C --> D[Channels]
    D --> E[News Content]
    E --> F[News Channels]
    
    G[Public API] --> E
    H[Admin API] --> E
    H --> C
    H --> D
```

### Data Flow Diagrams

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant Client
    participant API
    participant Supabase
    participant DB
    
    Client->>+API: GET /api/public/news
    API->>+Supabase: Query published news
    Supabase->>+DB: SELECT * FROM news WHERE status='published'
    DB-->>-Supabase: Return news data
    Supabase-->>-API: JSON response
    API-->>-Client: News feed
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Layout**: `/app/layout.tsx` - Root layout with metadata and global styling
- **Home Page**: `/app/page.tsx` - Landing page showcasing latest news
- **Dashboard**: `/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/page.tsx` - Admin interface for content management

### Core Modules
- **Data Layer**: `/lib/data.ts` - Centralized database operations with Supabase client
- **Type Definitions**: `/lib/types.ts` - TypeScript interfaces for all domain entities
- **Authentication**: `/lib/supabase/server.ts` - Server-side Supabase client configuration

### Configuration Approach
- **Build Configuration**: `/next.config.js` - Image optimization settings for Supabase storage
- **Package Dependencies**: `/package.json` - Modern React stack with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS
- **Environment Setup**: `/vercel-env.json` - Vercel deployment configuration

### External Dependencies
- **Next.js 16.2.1**: React framework with App Router
- **Supabase**: PostgreSQL database with authentication and real-time features
- **React 19.2.4**: Component-based UI library
- **Tailwind CSS 4.2.2**: Utility-first CSS framework
- **TypeScript 5.9.3**: Type-safe JavaScript development

### Integration Points
- **Supabase Authentication**: JWT-based user sessions
- **Supabase Realtime**: Database change notifications
- **Vercel Deployment**: Cloud-native hosting with edge network
- **External Website Integration**: Public API endpoints for third-party sites

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[NewsFeed Component] --> B[Public API]
    C[NewsWidget Component] --> B
    D[Dashboard Components] --> E[Admin API]
    E --> F[Supabase Client]
    F --> G[(Database)]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
- **Multi-channel Publishing**: Content can be distributed across multiple channels simultaneously
- **Collaborative Editing**: Team-based content creation with permission controls
- **Public API**: RESTful endpoints for external website integration
- **Real-time Updates**: Automatic content refresh across connected websites
- **Role-based Permissions**: Granular access control with inheritance

### Implementation Highlights
- **News Feed Component**: `/components/news-feed.tsx` - Flexible news display with configurable options
- **Channel Management**: `/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/channels/` - Administrative interface for channel configuration
- **News Creation Workflow**: `/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/news/new/` - Full CRUD operations for content management
- **Permission System**: `/lib/supabase/server.ts` - RBAC implementation with channel-specific access

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Draft: Create News
    Draft --> Published: Publish to Channels
    Published --> Hidden: Hide Content
    Hidden --> Published: Unhide
    Published --> Archived: Archive
    Draft --> Archived: Delete
    Archived --> [*]
```

### Data Model Architecture

```mermaid
erDiagram
    CHANNELS ||--o{ CHANNEL_EDITORS : has
    CHANNEL_EDITORS ||--|| USER_PROFILES : assigns
    CHANNELS ||--o{ NEWS : publishes
    USER_PROFILES ||--o{ NEWS : authors
    NEWS ||--o{ NEWS_CHANNELS : appears_in
    NEWS_CHANNELS ||--|| CHANNELS : distributed_to
```

## 5. Conclusion and Recommendations

### Project Architecture Summary
The Blog Management System demonstrates a well-structured, modern web application architecture that successfully combines:
- **Frontend Excellence**: Next.js App Router with React Server Components
- **Backend Simplicity**: Supabase as a managed backend service
- **Security First**: Multi-layered security including RLS and RBAC
- **Developer Experience**: TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and automated deployment

### Architectural Strengths
- **Scalability**: Cloud-native architecture with automatic scaling
- **Maintainability**: Clear separation of concerns with modular components
- **Security**: Comprehensive security layers from authentication to database policies
- **Flexibility**: Multiple integration patterns for different client types

### Potential Improvement Areas
- **Performance Optimization**: Implement caching strategies for frequently accessed news data
- **Content Versioning**: Add support for content revision history
- **Analytics Integration**: Track content performance and user engagement metrics
- **Media Management**: Enhanced image processing and CDN optimization

### Actionable Recommendations
1. **Implement Caching**: Add Redis caching for popular news endpoints
2. **Enhance Monitoring**: Integrate application performance monitoring (APM)
3. **Add Search**: Implement full-text search capabilities across news content
4. **Content Scheduling**: Extend publish scheduling beyond immediate publication
5. **Audit Logging**: Track all content changes for compliance and debugging

### Next Steps for Project Evolution
- **Mobile Application**: Develop native mobile apps for iOS and Android
- **Content Analytics**: Build dashboard for content performance metrics
- **Social Media Integration**: Automated social media posting from news content
- **Multi-language Support**: Expand internationalization capabilities

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [next.config.js](next.config.js)
- [app/layout.tsx](app/layout.tsx)
- [lib/types.ts](lib/types.ts)
- [lib/data.ts](lib/data.ts)
- [components/news-feed.tsx](components/news-feed.tsx)
- [app/api/public/news/route.ts](app/api/public/news/route.ts)
- [app/(dashboard)/dashboard/page.tsx](app/(dashboard)/dashboard/page.tsx)
- [supabase-schema.sql](supabase-schema.sql)
- [ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md)

</blog>
